message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "claim with more damage entries of a type than insured throws"
Passing tests so far: 21

Make `runScenario` throw when the number of damage entries for a given `itemType` exceeds the number of items in the policy with that `type`.

Example: policy has one sword. Claim has two `{itemType: "sword"}` entries. → throw.

Existing tests don't have this condition (single damage per item type, except none with duplicates). All previous tests must still pass.

Implementation: at the start of processClaim, count damages by itemType and policy items by type; if any damage-itemType has more damages than policy items, throw.

Run `pnpm test` to verify all 22 pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and test file, then add the minimal code needed to pass the failing test.
Now I'll add the minimal validation at the start of `processClaim`.

content.text:
All 22 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a `countBy` helper and a validation block at the start of `processClaim` that counts damages by `itemType` and policy items by `type`, then throws if any damage-itemType count exceeds the policy item count for that type.
- Result: All 22 tests pass (16 todo).
- Approach: Minimal addition that targets exactly the new test condition. Used a small `countBy` helper to keep the validation readable while not over-engineering. No changes to other code paths, so all previously passing tests remain green.

Relevant files:
